title: ModelInfo
description: Metadata for a single AI model as produced by the bestiary CLI JSON formatter.

Provider.description: Organization that hosts or publishes the model (e.g. "anthropic").

RawFamily.description: Raw API family field verbatim as returned by the upstream API (e.g. "claude-opus", "gemini-flash").

Family.description: Canonical family identifier extracted from RawFamily (or inferred from ID when RawFamily is empty). Populated at codegen time.

Variant.description: Variant suffix extracted from RawFamily (e.g. "opus-4", "pro", "flash"). Empty when the model has no variant. Populated at codegen time.

Version.description: Model version extracted from the family string (e.g. "4.5" for claude-opus-4-5, "2.5" for gemini-2.5-flash). Empty when the model has no separable version. Populated at codegen time.

Date.description: Release date extracted from the model ID or ReleaseDate field in YYYY-MM-DD format. Empty when no date is found. Populated at codegen time.

Modifier.description: List of known trailing tokens extracted from the model ID that carry semantic meaning beyond family/variant/version/date (e.g. ["thinking"], ["vision","instruct"]). Stored in deterministic canonical order (capability > speed > format/stage, alpha fallback). null when no known modifier is found. Widened from string to array of strings for lossless multi-modifier capture.

ContextWindow.description: Maximum number of tokens the model can process in a single request.

MaxOutput.description: Maximum number of tokens the model can generate in a single response.

Reasoning.description: Whether the model supports chain-of-thought or extended reasoning.

ToolCall.description: Whether the model supports tool/function calling.

Attachment.description: Whether the model supports file attachments.

Temperature.description: Whether the model exposes a temperature parameter.

StructuredOutput.description: Whether the model supports structured JSON output.

Interleaved.description: Polymorphic capability for interleaved reasoning. Supported indicates whether the capability is active; Config holds optional configuration (e.g. {"field": "reasoning_details"}).

Supported.description: Whether the interleaved capability is enabled.

Config.description: Optional capability configuration key-value pairs, or null if not applicable.

OpenWeights.description: Whether the model weights are publicly available.

CostInputPerMTok.description: Cost in USD per million input tokens, or null if unknown.

CostOutputPerMTok.description: Cost in USD per million output tokens, or null if unknown.

CostReasoningPerMTok.description: Cost in USD per million reasoning tokens, or null if unknown.

CostCacheReadPerMTok.description: Cost in USD per million cache-read tokens, or null if unknown.

CostCacheWritePerMTok.description: Cost in USD per million cache-write tokens, or null if unknown.

ReleaseDate.description: Model release date string as provided by the upstream API (e.g. "2024-01-01").

Knowledge.description: Knowledge cutoff date string as provided by the upstream API.

Modalities.description: Input and output modalities supported by the model.

LastSynced.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when this model record was last fetched from the upstream API.

ModelRef.title: ModelRef
ModelRef.description: The canonical identity of a model. The 7-field tuple (ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Version, Date) is the stable anchor for cross-provider queries, canonical formatting, and the normalization pipeline.

ID.description: Original API model identifier (e.g. "claude-opus-4-20250514"). Corresponds to the ModelID type in Go.

Provider.description: Organization that hosts or publishes the model (e.g. "anthropic").

RawFamily.description: API family field verbatim as returned by the upstream API (e.g. "claude-opus").

Family.description: Canonical family identifier after normalization (e.g. "claude"). Empty if not yet normalized.

Variant.description: Canonical variant suffix (e.g. "opus", "sonnet", "haiku"). Empty when the model has no variant.

Version.description: Model version extracted from the family string (e.g. "4.5" for claude-opus-4-5, "2.5" for gemini-2.5-flash). Empty when the model has no separable version.

Date.description: Release date in YYYY-MM-DD format extracted from the model ID. Empty if none was found.

Modifier.description: List of known trailing tokens extracted from the model ID that carry semantic meaning (e.g. ["thinking"], ["vision","instruct"]), in canonical order. null when none. Widened from string to array of strings to match ModelRef.Modifier []string.

CanonicalScheme.title: CanonicalScheme
CanonicalScheme.description: Identifies the string serialization format for a ModelRef. Controls how model identity is expressed as a string (e.g. for CLI output, SBOM generation, or HuggingFace Hub lookups).

x-enum-descriptions.canonical: Slash-separated canonical form: <provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>. Variant and date segments are omitted when empty. Falls back to <provider>/<raw-id> when Family is empty.
x-enum-descriptions.huggingface: HuggingFace Hub form: <provider>/<raw-id>. Matches the repo-id used by the HuggingFace Hub API.
x-enum-descriptions.raw: Original API model ID verbatim (ModelRef.ID as a string).

AcceptabilityRating.title: AcceptabilityRating
AcceptabilityRating.description: Classifies a Designation by its acceptability status, following ISO 1087 terminology principles. All designations generated in the normalization epoch default to 'admitted'. Promotion to 'preferred' and assignment of 'deprecated' are deferred to a follow-up curation epoch.

x-enum-descriptions.admitted: Default rating. The designation is recognized and may be used, but is not the preferred form.
x-enum-descriptions.preferred: The designation is the recommended form. No designations are promoted to Preferred in the current epoch.
x-enum-descriptions.deprecated: The designation is no longer recommended. No designations are deprecated in the current epoch.

Designation.title: Designation
Designation.description: Pairs a model identity string with its serialization scheme, hosting provider, and acceptability rating. A single ModelRef may have multiple Designations: a canonical form, a raw API ID form, and optionally provider-specific alias forms.

Value.description: The serialized model identifier under the given Scheme.

Scheme.description: The serialization scheme used to produce Value.

Provider.description: The hosting provider associated with this designation. For cross-scheme designations (e.g. raw) the Provider is the original model's hosting provider.

Rating.description: Classifies acceptability. All epoch-generated designations are 'admitted'; promotion is deferred.
